danger_signals.visual_form: ball control indicator and player positioning on isometric field
danger_signals.meaning: threat to goal; risk of conceding

danger_signals.visual_form: sparse player positioning near goal area
danger_signals.meaning: exposed defensive formation; danger of scoring opportunity for opponent

danger_signals.visual_form: free kick or penalty kick setup visualization
danger_signals.meaning: high-risk scoring opportunity for opposing team

safety_signals.visual_form: highlight or selection marker on controlled footballer
safety_signals.meaning: agency and ability to direct play; safe to execute offensive action

safety_signals.visual_form: ball held by player character; team formation cohesion
safety_signals.meaning: control of game tempo; reduced immediate threat

safety_signals.visual_form: multiple players positioned between ball and goal
safety_signals.meaning: defensive stability; reduced vulnerability to counter-attack

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: club badge/crest (fictional names used; no real player licenses)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: home stadium setting; crowd presence associated with home team

faction_indicators.color_identity: alternate kit color (contrasts with home team)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: away stadium setting; neutral or opposing crowd context

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: isometric field view with 22 players positioned according to formation and play direction
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: players move toward ball; positioning shifts based on possession and set-piece state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ball positioned at penalty mark or free kick location; players arranged in ceremonial formation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI-controlled players hold position; player gains execution window to attempt kick

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: match timer display; penalty shootout mode activation (new feature vs. previous game)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: transition from free play to sequential penalty kick execution; alternating team control

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: net animation; score display update on UI; visual celebration or reset
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: ball resets to center circle; teams reposition for kick-off

environmental_storytelling.example: clubs from six European leagues (Premier League, Ligue 1, Bundesliga, Serie A, Eredivisie, La Liga) plus Brazilian and US league clubs provide geographic/cultural identity through licensed league inclusion
home and away kit colors visually distinguish teams; club badges (though fictional due to licensing) serve as faction markers
previous game featured chanting/singing; FIFA '95 reduced this element, changing ambient audio storytelling to convey match atmosphere

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: responds to player input; highlighted on-field; can pass, shoot, or move directionally
AI-controlled; follows team formation and positioning logic; responds to ball state
positioning reflects shot threat; improved shot-stopping mechanics (noted as improvement over FIFA International Soccer)
forward movement toward goal; pressing behavior; increased threat signaling through positioning
withdrawal toward goal; formation tightening; reduced immediate threat posture

notes: FIFA Soccer 95 is a sports simulation with limited narrative world-state. Manifestation communication relies entirely on procedural gameplay state (ball possession, formation positioning, set-piece triggers) and visual kit/badge differentiation. No dynamic faction politics or danger escalation beyond match-state mechanics. Improvements in passing, set-pieces, and goalkeeping mechanics were gameplay-facing rather than manifestation-facing. Club licenses from six European leagues plus Brazil and US provide geographic/organizational faction identity, but player names are fictional (no portrait/likeness data). Isometric viewpoint standardizes communication across all match states. Penalty shootout system (new vs. previous game) introduces discrete world-state transition with distinct visual/behavioral manifestation.
